Headline: The Framework We Loved to Hate Just Killed Zone.js, Dropped RxJS Boilerplate, and Quietly Built the Fastest Frontend Engine on the Web.
Every few years, the frontend landscape undergoes a tectonic shift. For a long time, the narrative was set in stone: React is for agile, cutting-edge builders. Angular is for enterprise monoliths buried under endless boilerplate.
If you are still holding onto that 2020 mental model, I have some terrifying news for your codebase: You are missing the biggest architectural comeback in web development history.
With the release of Angular v22, the framework hasn’t just iterated — it has completely re-engineered what it means to build scalable web applications. By making Zoneless change detection the default, introducing production-ready Signal Forms, and launching native AI-driven refactoring tools, Angular has transformed into a blazing-fast, zero-bloat reactivity machine.
DRC Systems+ 1
Here is exactly why the “New Angular” is winning over hardcore React and Vue developers, and why your team might need to switch sooner than you think.
1. The Death of Zone.js (And the End of the 2-Second Boot Time)
